name: g15_transport_cocycle
purpose: Anti-drift transition law for carrying the walk description across overlaps of regions and lenses in the G15 transport shell.
core_statement: The cocycle is the lawful transition data that carries a local walk description from one region or lens to another while preserving the underlying transport event.

transition_classes.name: region_transition
transition_classes.description: Carries the walk state across overlaps between downstairs, stairs, and upstairs.

transition_classes.name: lens_transition
transition_classes.description: Re-expresses the same transport event in another visual lens without changing the underlying event.

transition_classes.name: stairs_transition
transition_classes.description: Special transition class centered on the stairs, where level changes and reorientation are most visible.

stairs_principle.statement: The stairs are the distinguished overlap region. In cube lens, the walk is read as starting and stopping on the stairs.

cocycle_effects.name: region_change
cocycle_effects.description: Updates the region label when the walk crosses between downstairs, stairs, and upstairs.

cocycle_effects.name: orientation_change
cocycle_effects.description: Updates local orientation when a crossing changes how forward/up/down are expressed.

cocycle_effects.name: register_shift
cocycle_effects.description: Carries the local register description into the next valid local chart.

cocycle_effects.name: lens_relabeling
cocycle_effects.description: Re-expresses anchors and local geometry in a new lens without altering the underlying transport event.

cocycle_effects.name: closure_transport
cocycle_effects.description: Carries sign/closure information consistently through local transitions.

invariants: The underlying transport event is preserved across a cocycle transition.
Changing lens does not change the theorem object.
Changing region changes local description, not transport identity.
The cocycle lives on overlaps, especially at the stairs.
anti_drift_rules: Do not treat the cocycle as a separate geometric object.
Do not confuse a lens change with a theorem-object renumbering.
Do not treat region transitions as arbitrary styling changes.
Do not detach stairs transitions from orientation change.

plain_language.house_picture: Downstairs and upstairs are different local floorplans. The stairs landing is where the translation happens. The cocycle is the rule that says how a walk state downstairs is re-expressed upstairs while remaining the same transport event.
plain_language.transport_picture: The cocycle is the glue law on overlaps. It tells you how the walk is rewritten when passing between sheets or between lenses.
